title: Bible Quiz - Questions by Book
description: Simple, scriptural, factual questions, 4 options per question, 1 or 2 correct answers.

questions.question: How many days does it take God to create the world according to Genesis 1?
On the seventh day, what does God do?
What is the name of the first man created by God?
What does God form the first woman from?
What is the name of the garden where Adam and Eve live?
What fruit is forbidden to Adam and Eve (name of the tree)?
options: The tree of the knowledge of good and evil

questions.question: Who are the two sons of Adam and Eve involved in the first murder? (2 answers)
Who kills his brother out of jealousy?
What is Abel's occupation?
How many days and nights does the flood's rain last according to the account?
What is the name of the man who builds the ark?
Which birds does Noah send out to check if the waters have receded? (2 answers)
What does the dove bring back the second time, a sign the waters are receding?
What sign does God place in the sky after the flood?
What is the name of the tower that men build and God confuses?
What is the name of Abraham's hometown?
What is the name of Abraham's wife?
What is the name of the son of Abraham and Sarah?
What is the name of the son of Abraham and Hagar, his servant?
On which mountain must Abraham offer Isaac as a sacrifice?
Which two cities are destroyed by fire and sulfur? (2 answers)
Who turns into a pillar of salt while fleeing Sodom?
What are the names of Isaac and Rebekah's twin sons? (2 answers)
For what dish does Esau sell his birthright?
What is the name of the wife Jacob loves most?
How many years in total does Jacob work for Laban to marry Rachel and Leah?
After wrestling with an angel, what new name does Jacob receive?
How many sons does Jacob have in total?
What is the name of Jacob's favorite son, to whom he gives a special coat?
What do Joseph's brothers do out of jealousy?
options: They sell him as a slave

questions.question: In Egypt, what important position does Joseph eventually hold?
What does Joseph interpret for Pharaoh?
What do the 7 fat cows and 7 lean cows in Pharaoh's dream symbolize?
options: 7 years of plenty and 7 years of famine

questions.question: What is the name of Jacob's father?
What is the name of Jacob's twin brother?
What is the name of Jacob's youngest son, Joseph's full brother?
What is the name of Jacob's father-in-law?
Where do Jacob and his family end up settling thanks to Joseph?
How many years does Joseph live according to Genesis?
Who is the victim of the first murder recorded in the Bible?
Who is the deliverer of the people of Israel out of Egypt?
Where does God first appear to Moses in a bush?
What is the name of Moses' brother who speaks on his behalf before Pharaoh?
How many plagues does God send upon Egypt?
What is the first plague of Egypt?
What is the last plague of Egypt?
What must the Israelites do to be spared during the last plague?
options: Mark their doorposts with a lamb's blood

questions.question: Which sea do the Israelites cross on dry ground while fleeing Egypt?
Who pursues the Israelites to the Red Sea?
What is the name of the miraculous food sent from heaven in the desert?
On which mountain does Moses receive the Ten Commandments?
How many commandments does God give Moses?
What are the Ten Commandments engraved on?
What does the people make while Moses is on the mountain?
What is the name of the sacred chest containing the tablets of the law?
What is the name of the sacred tent used for worship during the desert crossing?
Who is Moses' father-in-law, a priest of Midian?
What is the name of Moses and Aaron's sister?
In what object is baby Moses placed on the Nile to be saved?
Who rescues baby Moses from the Nile?
What is the name of the oppressed people enslaved in Egypt?
What does Moses repeatedly ask Pharaoh?
How many days and nights does Moses stay on the mountain to receive the law?
Who helps Moses hold up his arms during the battle against Amalek? (2 answers)
Who leads the Israelite army against Amalek while Moses prays on the hill?
What happens to Moses' face after speaking with God on the mountain?
Which precious metal is mainly used for the Ark of the Covenant?
How many of the commandments mainly concern the relationship with God (the first 4, general theme)?
What is the name of the desert where the Israelites receive the manna?
What means of transport does Pharaoh send to pursue the Israelites?
What does Moses do with his staff before Pharaoh as a first sign?
options: He turns it into a snake
He plants it in the ground

questions.question: How long had the Israelites lived in Egypt before the Exodus, according to the text?
What does God command to be eaten with the first Passover meal, besides the lamb? (2 answers)
What is the name of the craftsman filled with the Spirit of God to build the tabernacle?
What does Moses do upon coming down the mountain and seeing the golden calf?
options: He breaks the tablets of the law
He immediately returns up the mountain

questions.question: The book of Leviticus mainly concerns which tribe and its priestly functions?
Which annual day is described as the day of collective atonement?
On which day of the week must the people rest according to the law?
Which types of sacrifices are mentioned in Leviticus? (2 answers)
Which feast is celebrated seven weeks after Passover?
What is the name of the feast where the people live under tents/booths in remembrance of the desert?
Which commandment in Leviticus is quoted by Jesus as the second greatest commandment?
How many times a year does the high priest enter the Most Holy Place, according to the Day of Atonement?
Which sons of Aaron die for offering unauthorized fire to God? (2 answers)
Which year, every 50 years, frees slaves and returns land to its original owners?
Every how many years must the land rest (sabbatical year)?
Which animal must not be eaten according to the dietary laws of Leviticus (example)?
What must land animals have to be considered clean and edible?
options: A split hoof and chew the cud

questions.question: What is the name of the chief priest, brother of Moses, often mentioned in Leviticus?
Leviticus directly follows which book?
What does the title of the book of Numbers refer to?
How many spies does Moses send to explore Canaan?
Which two spies bring back a positive report about conquering Canaan? (2 answers)
How many years do the Israelites wander in the desert before entering Canaan?
Who rebels against the authority of Moses and Aaron and is swallowed by the earth?
What is the name of the pagan prophet hired by Balak to curse Israel, but who ends up blessing it?
Which animal speaks to Balaam in the account in Numbers?
What does Moses strike, instead of speaking to it, to bring forth water from a rock, which brings him a divine penalty?
options: He strikes it with his staff

questions.question: What consequence does Moses suffer for striking the rock instead of speaking to it?
options: He does not enter the promised land

questions.question: What is the name of the bronze serpent Moses raises in the desert to heal the people?
How many tribes of Israel are counted in the book of Numbers (excluding Levi, set apart)?
Which tribe is not counted in the military census because it is reserved for sanctuary service?
What is the name of Moses' sister struck with leprosy for opposing him?
Who is Moses' designated successor in the book of Numbers?
What famous priestly blessing is given to Aaron and his sons in Numbers?
options: The priestly blessing (The Lord bless you...)

questions.question: Deuteronomy is essentially made up of what?
options: Moses' speeches to the people before his death

questions.question: Where does Moses die, without entering the promised land?
Who becomes the leader of the people after Moses' death?
Which central declaration of Judaism is found in Deuteronomy ('Hear, O Israel...')?
How many years did Moses live according to Deuteronomy?
Deuteronomy repeats the Ten Commandments mainly in which chapter?
What does the word 'Deuteronomy' generally mean (sense of the title)?
options: The second law / repetition of the law

questions.question: From where can Moses only see the promised land without entering it?
options: From the top of Mount Nebo

questions.question: Which people does Moses remind of being delivered from Egypt to justify obedience to the law?
Which two things does Moses recall having received on Sinai/Horeb? (theme, 2 answers)
What central theme does Deuteronomy repeatedly stress about loving God 'with all your heart'?
options: It is a recurring central theme

questions.question: Who succeeds Moses to lead the people into the promised land?
Deuteronomy is part of which larger collection of biblical books?
What is the last book of the Pentateuch?
According to Deuteronomy, who buries Moses?
options: God himself (no one knows the place)

questions.question: Who succeeds Moses to conquer Canaan?
Which is the first city miraculously conquered, whose walls fall down?
What do the Israelites do around Jericho for 7 days before the walls fall?
What is the name of the woman in Jericho who hides the Israelite spies?
What sign does Rahab place in her window to be spared?
How many tribes divide the promised land under Joshua?
Which river do the Israelites cross miraculously to enter Canaan?
What does Joshua set up after crossing the Jordan as a memorial?
Who, among the spies, had given a positive report along with Joshua and inherits a special portion of land?
What is the name of the man punished for keeping forbidden plunder after the fall of Jericho?
At the end of his life, what famous declaration does Joshua make to the people?
options: 'As for me and my house, we will serve the Lord'

questions.question: The book of Joshua directly follows which book?
Which heavenly body does Joshua ask to stand still during a battle?
Approximately how long does the conquest of Canaan take under Joshua (general period)?
Which city, after Jericho, is taken after an initial military failure by the Israelites?
What does the role of the 'judges' mean in this biblical book?
What is the name of the only female judge of Israel mentioned in the book?
Who kills the enemy general Sisera by driving a tent peg into his head while he sleeps?
What is the name of the judge who defeats the Midianites with only 300 men?
What test does Gideon use with a wool fleece to confirm God's calling?
options: The test of dew on the fleece

questions.question: What is the name of the extremely strong judge, known for his exceptional physical strength?
Where does Samson's extraordinary strength come from?
options: An angel who always accompanies him

questions.question: What is the name of the woman who betrays Samson by revealing the secret of his strength?
Which animal does Samson kill with his bare hands?
How does Samson die at the end of his life?
options: By bringing down a Philistine temple

questions.question: Which recurring enemy people does Samson fight throughout his story?
How many braids/locks of hair does Samson have, according to the account (precise detail)?
The book of Judges describes a repeating cycle: sin, oppression, crying out for help, then what?
Which judge makes a rash vow that costs him the sacrifice of his daughter?
The book of Judges is located between which two biblical books?
From which country does Ruth come before settling with Naomi in Israel?
What is the name of Ruth's mother-in-law?
What famous statement does Ruth make expressing her loyalty to Naomi?
In whose field does Ruth go to glean grain to feed herself?
What is the name of the wealthy man who eventually marries Ruth?
Ruth and Boaz are the great-grandparents of which famous king of Israel?
In which town does the story of Ruth mainly take place?
What is the name of Ruth's sister-in-law who returns to live among her own people?
What is the name of Samuel's mother, who prays for a long time to have a child?
Under which priest does Samuel grow up and serve at the sanctuary?
How does God first call Samuel?
Who is the first king of Israel, anointed by Samuel?
Which young shepherd kills the Philistine giant Goliath?
With what simple weapon does David strike down Goliath?
What is the name of David's best friend, son of King Saul?
Why does King Saul become jealous of David?
options: His popularity after his military victories

questions.question: What is the name of the Philistine giant defeated by David?
From which tribe does Saul, the first king, come?
Who does Saul secretly consult before his last battle, despite the law's prohibition?
How does King Saul die?
options: He takes his own life in battle

questions.question: From which town does David come?
What is David's occupation before becoming famous?
Who secretly anoints David as future king while he is still a young shepherd?
How many of Jesse's sons are presented to Samuel before David is chosen?
David plays an instrument to soothe Saul: which one?
About how many times does David spare Saul's life when he could kill him?
What is the name of the wise woman who prevents David from taking revenge on Nabal?
What is the name of David's first wife, daughter of Saul?
Which king succeeds Saul over all Israel after a period of division?
Which city does David conquer to make his capital?
What is the name of the woman with whom David commits adultery?
What is the name of Bathsheba's husband, sent by David to certain death in battle?
Which prophet confronts David after his sin with Bathsheba?
What is the name of David's son who rebels against his father?
How does Absalom die during his flight after the battle?
options: His hair gets caught in a tree and he is killed

questions.question: Which sacred object does David bring to Jerusalem amid great celebration?
options: Only the tablets of the law

questions.question: David composes many texts found in which poetic biblical book?
Which son of David, born of Bathsheba, will succeed him as king?
What is the name of David's army commander, known for his loyalty but also his violence?
David dances before the Ark of the Covenant; which wife despises him for it?
How long does David reign in total over Israel, according to biblical tradition?
What covenant does God make with David concerning his descendants and his throne?
The book of 2 Samuel ends before the reign of which king?
Which son of David becomes king after him?
What does Solomon ask God for when offered the chance to have any wish granted?
Which great religious building does Solomon have built in Jerusalem?
Which famous foreign queen visits Solomon to test his wisdom?
How does Solomon settle the dispute between two women claiming the same baby?
options: He proposes cutting the child in two to reveal the true mother

questions.question: After Solomon's death, the kingdom splits into two: which ones? (2 answers)
Which prophet confronts the prophets of Baal on Mount Carmel?
What is the name of the foreign queen, wife of King Ahab, who promotes the worship of Baal?
How is Elijah miraculously fed by a brook?
Which widow shelters Elijah and sees her flour and oil never run out?
How many false prophets of Baal does Elijah confront on Mount Carmel?
How does Elijah leave the earth at the end of his life?
options: Taken up to heaven in a chariot of fire

questions.question: What is the name of Elijah's successor/disciple?
Who is the first king of the northern kingdom (Israel) after the division?
Solomon's son, whose harsh decisions lead to the division of the kingdom, is who?
About how long does it take Solomon to build the Temple of Jerusalem?
Solomon is credited with writing much of which book of wisdom?
Which king marries many foreign wives, leading his heart astray to other gods?
On which mountain does Elijah hear 'a still small voice' of God?
Which king of Israel (north), married to Jezebel, often opposes the prophet Elijah?
Which prophet receives a double portion of his master Elijah's spirit?
What is the name of the leprous Syrian general healed by the prophet Elisha?
What must Naaman do to be healed of his leprosy?
Which empire eventually destroys the northern kingdom (Israel) and deports its population?
Which empire eventually destroys the southern kingdom (Judah) and Jerusalem, leading to the Babylonian exile?
What is the name of the king of Judah known for his great religious reforms after the discovery of the book of the law?
Which king of Judah falls sick and then has his life extended by 15 years after a prayer?
How does Elisha multiply a widow's oil to pay her debts?
options: She fills all the available jars with a little oil
He prays and oil falls from the sky

questions.question: What sign accompanies Elijah's being taken up, witnessed by Elisha?
options: A chariot and horses of fire

questions.question: What major event does the book of 2 Kings end with?
options: The fall of Jerusalem and the exile to Babylon

questions.question: About how many kings succeed one another in the northern kingdom before its destruction (approximate number of unstable dynasties)?
Which prophet eats a scroll and prophesies the fall of Jerusalem, active during this later period (linked to the context of 2 Kings)?
The Temple of Jerusalem, built by Solomon, is destroyed by whom at the end of 2 Kings?
Which king of Judah is described as having done 'what was right in the eyes of the Lord', compared to David?
What is the name of the capital of the northern kingdom (Israel) before its fall?
The books of Chronicles largely cover the same period as which other books?
1 Chronicles begins with long lists of what?
Which king is especially highlighted in 1 Chronicles for his preparations for the Temple?
Who actually builds the Temple, according to 2 Chronicles?
2 Chronicles ends with which decree allowing the return from exile?
Which king of Judah is known in 2 Chronicles for repairing the Temple after finding it neglected?
Chronicles especially emphasizes which central theme related to worship?
How many books make up the Chronicles collection in the Bible?
Which very wicked king of Judah, according to the account, ends up repenting in captivity in Babylon (an account unique to Chronicles)?
Chronicles is classified in which major section of the Old Testament?
Which Persian king authorizes the return of the Jews from exile and the rebuilding of the Temple?
Which main building do the Jews rebuild under the leadership of Ezra and Zerubbabel?
What role does Nehemiah hold at the Persian court before leaving for Jerusalem?
What does Nehemiah mainly set out to rebuild in Jerusalem?
How many days does it take, according to the account, to complete the rebuilding of the walls under Nehemiah?
Ezra is mainly described as what?
Which notable public event takes place with Ezra reading the law before the whole assembled people?
options: The public reading of the law

questions.question: Who actively opposes the rebuilding of Jerusalem's walls under Nehemiah (example of a name)?
The books of Ezra and Nehemiah take place after which major event?
options: The building of the first Temple

questions.question: Under whose leadership does the first wave of exiles return to rebuild the Temple?
In which empire does the story of Esther take place?
What is the name of the king who chooses Esther as queen?
What is the family relationship between Esther and Mordecai?
options: He is her cousin and guardian

questions.question: Which high official plots to exterminate all the Jews of the empire?
Which previous queen is deposed before Esther's arrival?
What does Esther do before appearing before the king uninvited, risking her life?
Which Jewish feast commemorates the deliverance recounted in the book of Esther?
On what is Haman finally hanged, ironically what he had prepared for Mordecai?
A famous feature of the book of Esther is the absence of which word throughout the entire text?
What high position does Mordecai eventually hold at court, replacing Haman?
Job is described at the start of the book as a man who is what?
options: Poor and sick from the start

questions.question: Who tests Job with God's permission?
What does Job lose during his trials (general category)?
options: His possessions, his children, and his health

questions.question: How many friends come to comfort (and then argue with) Job?
options: Three main ones (then a younger fourth)

questions.question: Which physical illness afflicts Job?
At the end of the book, how does Job's situation turn out?
options: God restores and doubles his possessions
He remains ill for the rest of his life

questions.question: From what does God finally answer Job toward the end of the book?
Which category of biblical literature does the book of Job belong to?
How many daughters and sons does Job have again after his restoration?
What is the name of one of Job's three friends, often cited?
How many psalms does the book of Psalms contain?
Which king is traditionally associated with composing many of the psalms?
Psalm 23 opens with which famous image?
Which Hebrew word, often translated as a pause or musical notation, frequently occurs in the Psalms?
Psalm 1 compares the faithful man to what?
options: A tree planted by streams of water

questions.question: Which psalm is often identified as the shortest of the entire book?
Which psalm is often identified as the longest chapter in the entire Bible?
The word 'Hallelujah' (praise the Lord) appears frequently, especially in which psalms?
Psalm 150 calls for praising God with what? (general theme)
The Psalms are classified in which major biblical category?
Some psalms are attributed to a Levite choir leader, besides David: who is frequently cited?
Psalm 22 is often read in connection with which theme of suffering?
Which psalm evokes forgiveness after David's sin with Bathsheba (a psalm of repentance)?
The book of Psalms is traditionally divided into how many large parts/books?
What is the main theme of Psalm 100, often used for collective praise?
Psalm 91 is known for its theme of what?
About how many psalms carry the heading 'Of David' in the traditional Hebrew text?
Psalm 8 particularly marvels at what?
options: The greatness of God in creation and mankind's place

questions.question: Which stringed instrument is most often mentioned in connection with the Psalms and David?
The word 'Psalm' comes from a Greek term originally meaning what?
options: A song accompanied by a stringed instrument

questions.question: To which king is most of the book of Proverbs attributed?
To which category of biblical literature does Proverbs belong?
The book of Proverbs opens by stating that what is the beginning of wisdom?
The final chapter of Proverbs (31) is famous for describing whom?
Proverbs contains maxims partly attributed to another named sage, besides Solomon: who?
According to Proverbs, what is better to have than gold and silver, according to a famous verse?
How many chapters does the book of Proverbs have?
Proverbs often uses the image of two opposing paths: which ones? (2 answers)
Which insect is cited as an example of hard work and foresight in Proverbs?
Proverbs is located between which two books in the usual biblical order?
Ecclesiastes is traditionally attributed to whom?
Which word recurs very often in Ecclesiastes to describe the vanity/fleeting nature of things?
Ecclesiastes states there is a time for everything: a time to be born and a time to what?
Which category of biblical literature does Ecclesiastes belong to?
What overall conclusion does Ecclesiastes give at the end of the book about man's duty?
options: Fear God and keep his commandments

questions.question: The Song of Songs is traditionally attributed to whom?
The Song of Songs is mainly a poem about which theme?
Which category of biblical literature does the Song of Songs belong to?
Isaiah carries out his prophetic ministry mainly in which kingdom?
The book of Isaiah contains a famous prophecy announcing the birth of a child called what?
Isaiah sees a vision of God in the Temple surrounded by which heavenly creatures?
Which Persian king is mentioned by name in Isaiah as a future deliverer of the exiles?
Isaiah is often cited in the New Testament regarding whom?
Approximately how many chapters does the book of Isaiah, a major prophetic book, have?
Isaiah compares the unfaithful people to what in a famous parable in chapter 5?
Which major biblical category does the book of Isaiah belong to?
Isaiah receives his inaugural vision in the year of the death of which king of Judah?
What burning coal touches Isaiah's mouth to purify his lips during his vision?
options: A live coal taken from the altar

questions.question: Jeremiah is often nicknamed what, because of the sadness of his writings?
Jeremiah prophesies before and during the fall of which city?
Which object does Jeremiah use as an image to show that God shapes his people?
Jeremiah wears a wooden yoke to symbolize what?
Which biblical book of sorrowful poetry is traditionally attributed to Jeremiah, written after the fall of Jerusalem?
Jeremiah announces a new covenant that God will write where?
Jeremiah is thrown into what by his opponents before being rescued?
How many years does Jeremiah announce that the exile to Babylon will last?
Which biblical category does the book of Jeremiah belong to?
What is the name of the scribe who writes down Jeremiah's dictated words?
Ezekiel carries out his prophetic ministry mainly where?
Ezekiel has a famous vision of a valley filled with what, which come back to life?
What does the vision of the valley of dry bones symbolize?
Ezekiel sees an inaugural vision with heavenly creatures and wheels: how are they described?
options: Wheels within wheels, with living creatures

questions.question: Which biblical category does the book of Ezekiel belong to?
Ezekiel must perform symbolic acts to illustrate his messages: which one is given as an example?
Ezekiel is sometimes called 'son of' what by God throughout the book?
Ezekiel also prophesies against several foreign nations, including which one?
Daniel and his companions are taken into exile in which empire?
What are the names of Daniel's three companions thrown into the fiery furnace?
Why are Daniel's three companions thrown into the furnace?
options: They refuse to worship the king's golden statue

questions.question: In which den is Daniel thrown for continuing to pray to his God?
What does King Nebuchadnezzar see in his famous dream interpreted by Daniel (a statue)?
options: A statue made of different metals

questions.question: Which king sees a mysterious hand writing on the wall during a feast?
What do the words written on the wall mean, according to Daniel's interpretation (general sense)?
options: The imminent fall of the kingdom

questions.question: What does Daniel refuse to defile himself with at the Babylonian court at the beginning of the book?
Which biblical category does the book of Daniel belong to?
Daniel refuses to bow to an idol despite a royal decree forbidding prayer to any god but the king: how many times a day does he pray as usual?
Who shuts the lions' mouths to protect Daniel according to the account?
options: No one, the lions are asleep

questions.question: Daniel interprets dreams mainly for which Babylonian king?
How does the story describe Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego coming out of the furnace?
options: They come out unharmed, without any burns

questions.question: What is the name of the king who signs the decree sending Daniel to the lions' den?
At the beginning of the book, Daniel is described as part of which group brought to serve at the royal court?
options: Young Israelite nobles chosen to serve the king

questions.question: Which prophet is swallowed by a great fish after fleeing his mission?
To which city does God send Jonah to preach, though he tries to escape it?
To which city does Jonah flee by boat instead of obeying God?
How long does Jonah stay in the belly of the fish?
The prophet Hosea is commanded to marry an unfaithful woman to illustrate what?
What was the prophet Amos's occupation before being called by God?
Amos especially denounces which social injustice in his prophecies?
The prophet Micah announces the birth of the Messiah in which small town?
The book of Joel describes a devastating invasion of which insects as a sign of judgment?
The short book of Obadiah is mainly a prophecy against which people?
The prophet Habakkuk mainly dialogues with God about what problem?
options: The problem of injustice and God's silence

questions.question: The book of Zephaniah mainly announces which central theme?
options: The day of the Lord (coming judgment)

questions.question: The prophet Haggai especially encourages the people to do what after the exile?
The prophet Zechariah has many symbolic visions and announces the coming of a king how?
What is the very last book of the Old Testament in the usual Christian order?
What does the Gospel of Matthew begin with?
options: The birth of John the Baptist

questions.question: In which town is Jesus born according to Matthew?
Who comes to pay homage to the infant Jesus, guided by a star?
Which king, frightened by Jesus' birth, orders the massacre of the children of Bethlehem?
Where does Jesus' family flee to escape Herod?
Who baptizes Jesus in the Jordan?
For how many days does Jesus fast in the desert before being tempted?
What is the name of Jesus' famous discourse containing the Beatitudes?
How many loaves and fish does Jesus multiply to feed 5,000 people?
How many apostles does Jesus choose?
What is the name of the apostle who betrays Jesus?
For how many pieces of silver does Judas hand over Jesus?
What is the name of the garden where Jesus prays before his arrest?
What is the name of the place where Jesus is crucified?
How many times does Peter deny Jesus before the rooster crows?
Which Roman governor sentences Jesus to crucifixion?
What does the angel say at Jesus' resurrection, according to Matthew?
options: 'He is not here, he has risen'

questions.question: About how many parables about the Kingdom of Heaven does Jesus tell in Matthew 13?
options: Several (e.g. the sower, the weeds, the pearl...)

questions.question: Which apostle briefly walks on water toward Jesus before doubting?
Which model prayer does Jesus teach his disciples in the Sermon on the Mount?
After his resurrection, what great command does Jesus give his disciples (the Great Commission)?
options: Go and make disciples of all nations

questions.question: How many talents (a unit of currency) are given to the three servants in the parable of the talents?
What sign do the wise men follow to find Jesus?
In which region of Galilee does Jesus mainly grow up?
What occupation did Peter and Andrew have before following Jesus?
The Gospel of Mark is generally considered to have what kind of style?
options: Brief and fast-paced, rich in action

questions.question: Mark begins his gospel directly with which event, without a genealogy?
options: The ministry of John the Baptist

questions.question: Who baptizes Jesus at the beginning of the Gospel of Mark?
In Mark, how many disciples does Jesus call to form his inner circle?
Which storm does Jesus calm in front of his frightened disciples?
options: A storm on the lake (Sea of Galilee)

questions.question: What is the name of the lake where much of Jesus' ministry takes place according to Mark?
Mark describes Jesus healing many sick people: which type of miracle appears very often?
Who is the first, in Mark, to recognize that Jesus is the Christ, during a confrontation at Caesarea Philippi?
On which mountain is Jesus transfigured before three disciples?
Which three disciples witness the transfiguration of Jesus? (2 answers among these)
What is the name of the place where Jesus is crucified according to Mark?
In Mark, who helps Jesus carry his cross?
Who asks for Jesus' body to bury it after the crucifixion?
Which women discover the empty tomb according to Mark (example of a name)?
Mark is traditionally associated with which apostle as the main source for his account?
The Gospel of Luke opens with the announcement of whose birth, before that of Jesus?
What are the names of John the Baptist's parents according to Luke?
Which angel announces to Mary that she will bear Jesus?
Where is Jesus born according to Luke, for lack of room elsewhere?
Who are the first to come see the infant Jesus in the fields, alerted by angels?
What is the name of Luke's famous parable about a man helping an injured stranger?
What is the name of the parable about the son who squanders his inheritance and then returns to his father?
Which short man, a tax collector, climbs a tree to see Jesus pass by?
Which tree does Zacchaeus climb to see Jesus?
How many disciples does Jesus send out on mission besides the twelve, according to Luke?
On the road to Emmaus after the resurrection, how many disciples walk with Jesus without recognizing him at first?
Which woman, in a famous scene, chooses to listen to Jesus while her sister is busy with household tasks?
Luke is traditionally identified as also having which profession?
Luke is also the author of a second biblical book that continues his gospel: which one?
In Luke, at what age is Jesus found in the Temple discussing with the teachers of the law?

options: One of the two criminals crucified with him

questions.question: About how much time passes between the birth of John the Baptist and that of Jesus, according to Luke's indications?
The Gospel of John begins with which famous expression?
What is the first miracle of Jesus recorded by John?
options: Water turned to wine at Cana

questions.question: At which celebration does Jesus turn water into wine?
What is the name of the Pharisee who comes to question Jesus by night?
Jesus talks with a woman at a well, in which region?
What is the name of Jesus' friend whom he raises after four days in the tomb?
What are the names of Lazarus' two sisters?
At the Last Supper, what does Jesus do for his disciples as a sign of humility?
options: He blesses them one by one in silence

questions.question: Who doubts the resurrection of Jesus until he sees his wounds?

John concludes his gospel saying Jesus did many other things not written down, for what purpose was the book written?
options: So that readers believe Jesus is the Christ
To tell the history of Israel

questions.question: How many days after his death does Jesus rise again, according to common gospel tradition?
options: Only the first day, at night

questions.question: John places the start of Jesus' public ministry after which event?
options: His baptism by John the Baptist

questions.question: Which well does Jesus use to speak with the Samaritan woman?

What title does Thomas give the risen Jesus upon recognizing him?
The book of Acts of the Apostles follows which gospel, by the same author?
What notable event occurs at Pentecost in Acts 2?
options: The descent of the Holy Spirit on the disciples

options: The Philippian jailer (name not given, converted with his family)

questions.question: What is the name of the Roman centurion, the first pagan explicitly mentioned as converted in Acts?
How does the book of Acts end for Paul?
options: Under house arrest in Rome, preaching freely

options: The inclusion of non-Jews (Gentiles) in the Church
The building of a new temple
The choice of a new apostle

options: Freedom through grace, not through the law
Only the imminent return of Christ

No James of the New Testament
